userStories.title: Centralized input sanitization middleware for all edge functions
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want a shared input sanitization layer applied to every edge function so that injection attacks (SQL, command, template) are blocked before reaching business logic. Shannon-style tools trace data flows from user input to dangerous sinks — this breaks those chains at the boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Create functions/_shared/sanitize.ts with sanitizeInput(), sanitizeSQL(), sanitizeHTML(), sanitizeCommand() utilities
All edge functions import and apply sanitizeInput() to request body/params before processing
SQL-like patterns (UNION SELECT, DROP TABLE, OR 1=1, etc.) are stripped or rejected with 400
Command injection characters (;, |, &&, $(), backticks) are escaped in any string passed to exec-like operations
Unit tests cover OWASP injection cheat sheet payloads (minimum 30 test vectors)
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Defense against Shannon's injection exploitation phase. Shannon traces data flows from input to dangerous operations — this sanitization layer breaks every flow at the boundary.

userStories.title: Audit and enforce parameterized queries across all Supabase calls
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want every database query to use parameterized inputs (never string interpolation) so that SQL injection is structurally impossible, even if sanitization is bypassed.
acceptanceCriteria: Grep entire codebase for string-interpolated Supabase queries (template literals in .from(), .rpc(), raw SQL)
Convert all interpolated queries to use Supabase client parameterized methods (.eq(), .in(), .filter(), etc.)
Add ESLint rule or custom lint script that flags raw SQL string concatenation/interpolation
Audit all .rpc() calls to ensure parameters are passed as function arguments, not embedded in SQL
Document findings in a SECURITY_AUDIT.md with before/after for each fix
All existing tests still pass
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon's code analysis identifies parameterized vs interpolated queries. Parameterized queries make injection structurally impossible regardless of input content.

userStories.title: Implement DOMPurify sanitization on all dynamic HTML rendering
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all user-generated or API-returned HTML to be sanitized through DOMPurify before rendering, so that stored and reflected XSS payloads are neutralized. Shannon tests XSS by injecting payloads through browser automation.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and configure DOMPurify as the standard HTML sanitization library
Audit all uses of dangerouslySetInnerHTML across the codebase and wrap each with DOMPurify.sanitize()
Audit TipTap blog editor output rendering — ensure stored blog HTML is sanitized on display
Audit all locations where API response data is rendered as HTML (AI-generated content, recipe instructions, etc.)
Create a shared SafeHTML component: <SafeHTML html={content} /> that applies DOMPurify automatically
Add unit tests with OWASP XSS cheat sheet payloads (<script>, onerror, javascript:, data:text/html, SVG payloads)
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon's XSS agent injects real payloads via browser automation. DOMPurify at every rendering boundary neutralizes stored, reflected, and DOM-based XSS.

userStories.title: Implement strict Content Security Policy without unsafe-inline
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want a strict CSP that eliminates unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval so that even if an XSS payload reaches the browser, it cannot execute. This is the critical layer that makes US-035 actionable.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace all inline <script> tags with external script files or nonce-based CSP
Replace all inline style= attributes in components with Tailwind classes or CSS modules

Configure CSP header with: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}'; style-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}'; img-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src 'self' https://api.tryeatpal.com https://functions.tryeatpal.com
Add CSP nonce generation to Cloudflare Pages _headers or _worker.js
Ensure Three.js, Framer Motion, GSAP, and Lottie still work under the new CSP
Add CSP violation reporting endpoint (report-uri or report-to directive)
Test in report-only mode first, then enforce after 1 week with no breakage
npm run build succeeds and all E2E tests pass
userStories.notes: Supersedes US-035. A strict CSP is the strongest browser-side XSS defense. Shannon's XSS payloads become inert even if they reach the DOM.

userStories.title: Implement URL allowlist validation service for all server-side fetches
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all server-side HTTP requests (fetch, axios, etc.) to pass through a URL validation service that blocks requests to internal/private IP ranges, so that SSRF attacks cannot reach internal infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create functions/_shared/url-validator.ts with validateExternalUrl() that rejects: private IPs (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.x), IPv6 loopback/link-local, cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), file:// and other non-http(s) schemes
Resolve DNS before validation to prevent DNS rebinding attacks (resolve hostname, check resulting IP is not private)
Apply validateExternalUrl() to: parse-recipe URL fetching, update-blog-image URL fetching, any OpenAI/external API proxy calls, grocery delivery API calls
Add timeout of 10s on all external fetches to prevent slow-loris style SSRF
Unit tests cover: localhost variations (127.0.0.1, 0.0.0.0, [::1], 0x7f000001), DNS rebinding scenarios, cloud metadata endpoints, non-HTTP schemes
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Builds on US-004. Shannon's SSRF agent attempts to force internal requests through URL manipulation, DNS rebinding, and IP encoding tricks. This blocks all known SSRF bypass techniques.

userStories.title: Implement CSRF protection on all state-changing operations
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want CSRF tokens validated on every state-changing request so that cross-origin form submissions and fetch requests cannot perform actions on behalf of authenticated users.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement CSRF token generation and validation using Supabase session-bound tokens or a double-submit cookie pattern
All POST/PUT/DELETE edge function endpoints validate CSRF token presence and correctness
Frontend automatically attaches CSRF token to all mutating API requests via a shared fetch wrapper or Supabase client interceptor
Token rotation occurs on each session refresh
Requests without valid CSRF token return 403 Forbidden with descriptive error
Unit tests verify: missing token rejected, invalid token rejected, expired token rejected, valid token accepted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent tests for CSRF by attempting cross-origin state-changing requests. CSRF tokens make these attacks impossible.

userStories.title: Harden session management and cookie security

Implement session ID rotation on login (new session ID after successful authentication)
Implement session ID rotation on privilege escalation (e.g., switching to admin views)
Add absolute session timeout (24h) and idle timeout (2h) with configurable values
Invalidate all other sessions when user changes password
Add session listing in user profile so users can see/revoke active sessions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent tests session fixation, cookie theft, and session replay. Proper session management eliminates these vectors.

userStories.title: Implement progressive rate limiting and account lockout on auth endpoints

Implement account-based rate limiting: 5 failed attempts trigger 15-min lockout, 10 trigger 1-hour lockout, 20 trigger 24-hour lockout with email notification
Rate limiting applies to: /auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up, /auth/reset-password, /auth/verify-otp, all OAuth callback endpoints
Add Cloudflare rate limiting rules as first line of defense before edge functions
Return generic 'Invalid credentials' message (never reveal if email exists)
Log all failed auth attempts with IP, user-agent, timestamp for security monitoring

userStories.notes: Extends US-036. Shannon handles complex auth flows including 2FA. Progressive rate limiting stops brute-force and credential stuffing regardless of how sophisticated the attack tool is.

userStories.title: Deploy comprehensive security headers via Cloudflare Pages
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all HTTP responses to include defense-in-depth security headers so that the browser enforces multiple protection layers against common web attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update public/_headers (Cloudflare Pages) with all security headers
Headers include: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
Headers include: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Headers include: X-Frame-Options: DENY
Headers include: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
Headers include: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), payment=(self)
Headers include: X-XSS-Protection: 0 (disabled in favor of CSP per modern best practice)
Headers include: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin
Headers include: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin
Verify headers present on production deployment using securityheaders.com (target A+ rating)

userStories.notes: Security headers are passive defenses that cost nothing at runtime. They prevent framing attacks, MIME sniffing, information leakage, and cross-origin exploitation — all vectors Shannon probes.

userStories.title: Implement security event monitoring and real-time alerting

acceptanceCriteria: Create a security_events table in Supabase to log: failed auth attempts, rate limit triggers, WAF blocks, CSRF validation failures, input sanitization rejections, suspicious user-agent patterns, unusual API usage patterns
Each event includes: timestamp, IP address, user_id (if authenticated), event_type, severity, request_path, request_method, user_agent, geo_location

Implement real-time alerting thresholds: >10 failed auths from same IP in 5 minutes → alert, >5 injection attempts from same IP in 1 minute → alert + auto-block, >3 SSRF attempts from any IP → alert + auto-block
Alert delivery via: Sentry (error tracking), email notification to security team, Slack webhook (if configured)
Add admin dashboard widget showing security events timeline and top blocked IPs
Implement auto-block: IPs triggering CRITICAL alerts are added to Cloudflare blocklist via API for 24 hours
Retain security events for 90 days minimum
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon runs for 1-1.5 hours generating hundreds of attack requests. Real-time monitoring would detect this pattern within minutes and auto-block the attacker IP.

userStories.title: Add bot detection and browser fingerprinting for automated attack prevention
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want automated browser-based attacks (like Shannon's headless Chrome exploitation) to be detected and challenged, so that automated pentesting tools cannot complete exploitation flows undetected.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrate Cloudflare Turnstile (free CAPTCHA alternative) on: login form, sign-up form, password reset form, contact/feedback forms

Implement browser fingerprinting checks on sensitive actions: detect headless Chrome indicators (navigator.webdriver, missing plugins, automation flags), detect rapid sequential form submissions (>3 submissions in 10 seconds), detect non-human mouse movement patterns on critical forms

Implement progressive challenges: first suspicious request gets Turnstile challenge, second gets rate limited, third gets IP blocked for 1 hour
Log all bot detection events to security_events table from US-099
Ensure accessibility: all CAPTCHA alternatives must be screen-reader accessible
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon uses headless browser automation for exploitation. Bot detection and browser fingerprinting add friction that forces attackers to use more sophisticated (and detectable) evasion techniques.

userStories.title: Remove debug endpoints, development artifacts, and source maps from production builds

Ensure production error boundaries show generic messages (never stack traces, SQL errors, or internal paths)
Verify source maps are NOT deployed to public CDN (only uploaded to Sentry for error tracking)

userStories.title: Implement strict API schema validation on all edge function endpoints
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want every edge function to validate request payloads against a strict Zod schema, rejecting any unexpected fields or types, so that type confusion and unexpected input attacks are blocked.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Zod schemas for every edge function's expected input in functions/_shared/schemas/
Every edge function validates request body against its schema as the first operation after auth check

Add maximum string length limits to all text fields (e.g., name: 200 chars, description: 5000 chars, URL: 2048 chars)
Add maximum array length limits to all array fields (prevent resource exhaustion)
Add maximum nesting depth to all object fields (prevent JSON parsing DoS)
Unit tests verify each schema rejects malformed input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Strict schema validation is the most effective input defense. Shannon tests with unexpected types, extra fields, and oversized payloads. Zod schemas reject all of these structurally.

userStories.title: Implement Subresource Integrity (SRI) for all external scripts and stylesheets

acceptanceCriteria: Audit all <script> and <link> tags that load resources from external CDNs
Add integrity= attribute with SHA-384 or SHA-512 hash to each external resource
Add crossorigin='anonymous' attribute alongside SRI attributes
Configure Vite build to generate SRI hashes for all chunk files
Add build-time validation that all external resources have SRI hashes
Document any external resources that cannot use SRI (e.g., dynamic APIs) with risk justification
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: SRI prevents supply chain attacks where a compromised CDN serves malicious scripts. While Shannon doesn't directly test this, it's a defense-in-depth measure against code injection via third-party resources.

userStories.title: Implement IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) protection audit

Verify each endpoint either: relies on RLS policy that filters by auth.uid(), or explicitly checks resource ownership before returning/modifying data
Test each endpoint with valid auth token but another user's resource IDs — all must return 403 or empty result
Audit all Supabase .select() calls in frontend to ensure they include user_id filter (defense in depth beyond RLS)
Add integration tests that create two test users and verify cross-user access is impossible for all resource types
Document IDOR audit findings and fixes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent specifically tests for authorization bypass by manipulating resource identifiers. IDOR is one of the most common and impactful web vulnerabilities.

userStories.title: Implement security regression test suite that runs on every PR

acceptanceCriteria: Create src/test/security/ directory with dedicated security test files

Each test file reproduces the original attack vector and verifies it is blocked
Add npm run test:security:unit that runs only security tests (fast, no external dependencies)
Integrate into GitHub Actions CI — security tests run on every PR alongside regular tests

Add test coverage for every US-085 through US-104 acceptance criterion that can be unit tested

Add width and height attributes to tags in src/pages/Auth.tsx (both Logo-Green.png and Logo-White.png, h-10 = 40px)
Add width and height attributes to tags in src/components/AppSidebar.tsx

userStories.title: Use <picture> elements with WebP sources for logos in Footer, Navigation, Auth, and AppSidebar
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want optimized image formats served so that pages load faster and use less bandwidth (Logo-Green.png is 261KB vs Logo-Green.webp at 28KB).
acceptanceCriteria: Replace bare with in src/components/Footer.tsx
Apply the same <picture> pattern for Logo-White.png/Logo-White.webp in Footer.tsx
Apply the same <picture> pattern in src/components/Navigation.tsx for both logos
Apply the same <picture> pattern in src/pages/Auth.tsx for both logos

MealSlotCard has .dropDestination that creates a PlanEntry(foodId: dropped.id, date: isoString, mealSlot: slot.rawValue, kidId: activeKidId). isTargeted state drives a green overlay border and a swap-in 'Release to add to X' label. Donates TipEvents.didDragFood on successful drop. Not yet verified on device — TabView-level drop targets (drag-hover-over-tab-to-switch) NOT implemented in this pass; user must navigate to destination tab before dropping.

userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 2: DONE 2026-04-17 (first pass). New Views/Scanner/DataScannerRepresentable.swift wraps VisionKit DataScannerViewController with a Configuration struct (barcodes + text toggles, quality level, highlighting, guidance) and delegate callbacks for didTapOn, didAdd, didRemove, and becameUnavailableWithError. New Views/Grocery/TextImportGrocerySheet.swift reviews recognised text with an editable transcript (live re-parse on edit) and the same parsed-items preview used by voice/photo paths. GroceryView's quick-add menu gained a 'Scan a list' entry that opens UnifiedScannerView in .groceryList mode then presents TextImportGrocerySheet with sourceTag: 'scan'. Follow-ups (not in this pass): recipe-card mode, ingredient-label mode, receipt mode, TipKit pointer at the scanner. Not yet verified on device.

userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 2: DONE 2026-04-17. New Intents/ folder with: GroceryAppIntents.swift (AddGroceryItemIntent — item: String, pipes through GroceryTextParser so 'two pounds of chicken' resolves qty/unit/category; WhatsForDinnerIntent — fetches today's plan_entries with dinner slot across all kids, resolves via DataService.shared.fetchFoods/fetchRecipes/fetchKids, composes a friendly dialog). Not yet implemented: intent donation from manual-equivalent actions (AddGroceryItemView form save, PlanEntryRow result log) and full kid-selection entity for multi-kid families. New EatPalShareExtension Xcode target in project.yml with app-extension type, com.eatpal.app.share bundle id, SKIP_INSTALL YES, SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY env-substituted in generated Info.plist, NSExtensionActivationSupportsWebURLWithMaxCount=1 + NSExtensionActivationSupportsText=true, NSExtensionPrincipalClass=$(PRODUCT_MODULE_NAME).ShareViewController. Added EatPalShareExtension.entitlements with group.com.eatpal.app. New Shared/PendingRecipeImport.swift defines Codable struct + PendingRecipeImportQueue (load/enqueue/remove/clear) backed by App Group UserDefaults key 'pending_recipe_imports'. AppState.drainPendingRecipeImports() called after loadAllData successfully completes — iterates queue, constructs Recipe (sourceType='share_extension'), calls addRecipe, removes only successful items from queue (failed stay for next launch). userStories.title: iOS: HealthKit nutrition write-back for logged meals
userStories.description: As a health-conscious parent, I want logged meals to write nutrition (energy, protein, carbs, fat) to my kid's Health profile, so EatPal becomes the authoritative nutrition source.
acceptanceCriteria: Add HealthKit capability to ios/EatPal/EatPal.entitlements
Add NSHealthShareUsageDescription and NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription to Info.plist
New HealthKitService.swift with request/check authorization for dietaryEnergyConsumed, dietaryProtein, dietaryCarbohydrates, dietaryFatTotal
When a plan_entry is logged as 'ate', compute macros from linked Food/Recipe and write an HKCorrelation for the meal slot

userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 2: DONE 2026-04-17. EatPal.entitlements gained com.apple.developer.healthkit (true). Info.plist gained NSHealthShareUsageDescription + NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription. New Services/HealthKitService.swift with isAvailable (HKHealthStore.isHealthDataAvailable), isEnabled (UserDefaults-backed user opt-in distinct from system authorization), requestAuthorization async, writeTypes covering dietaryEnergyConsumed / dietaryProtein / dietaryCarbohydrates / dietaryFatTotal + the .food correlation type, and writeMeal(calories:proteinGrams:carbsGrams:fatGrams:mealDate:mealName:) which bundles macros into one HKCorrelation so Health shows a single meal entry (not four orphan samples). Metadata uses HKMetadataKeyFoodType = mealName, no PII. AppState.updatePlanEntry now fires Task { await writeHealthSample(for:) } when result becomes MealResult.ate.rawValue and the entry has a linked recipe with nutritionInfo. New Views/Settings/HealthSettingsView.swift provides the toggle UI — flipping on triggers requestAuthorization, shows 'Open Settings' alert on denial, links to the Health app with x-apple-health:// URL. SettingsView got a new Preferences row 'Sync to Health' navigating into HealthSettingsView. Food-only plan entries (no linked recipe with nutrition) are silently skipped. Not yet verified on device.

userStories.title: iOS: Finish OfflineStore as durable write queue + wire NetworkMonitor
userStories.description: As an iOS user, I want edits I make offline (add to grocery, log meal, +1 pantry) to sync automatically when I'm back online, so I can use the app on a plane or in a store basement.
acceptanceCriteria: Promote ios/EatPal/EatPal/Services/OfflineStore.swift from placeholder to durable append-only write queue on disk (JSONEncoder to Application Support directory)
DataService writes route through OfflineStore first when NetworkMonitor reports offline or when a network call fails

Queue visible in Settings (diagnostic view showing pending count + last error)
Unit tests for queue encode/decode, flush ordering, failure retry in EatPalTests
Swift builds cleanly
userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 4: DONE 2026-04-17 (first pass). OfflineStore promoted to a durable SwiftData-backed write queue with typed enqueue helpers (enqueueInsert<T: Encodable>, enqueueUpdate, enqueueDelete) keyed by Table enum (foods / kids / recipes / plan_entries / grocery_items). syncPendingMutations now dispatches per-table and decodes JSON payloads back into domain structs, calling client.from(table).insert/update/delete. Sent Sentry breadcrumbs + error capture on sync failure. Published pendingMutationCount + isSyncing + lastSyncError so the UI can surface diagnostics. NetworkMonitor now tracks offline→online transitions and drains the queue automatically (`if !wasConnected && nowConnected { await OfflineStore.shared.syncPendingMutations() }`). AppState.addGroceryItem / deleteGroceryItem / toggleGroceryItem gained an isNetworkError(_:) classifier (URLError / NSURLErrorDomain codes) — on network errors the optimistic state is preserved and the mutation enqueued with a 'Queued for sync' toast. Remaining grocery + plan + food mutation methods can be retrofitted with the same pattern. Not yet verified on device.

userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-20 (first pass, no device verification). Scaffolded `android-native/` with settings.gradle.kts + build.gradle.kts + libs.versions.toml + app/build.gradle.kts (SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY/SENTRY_DSN BuildConfig wiring), proguard rules for kotlinx.serialization + supabase-kt, AndroidManifest with INTERNET / ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE / POST_NOTIFICATIONS + deep link intent filter for tryeatpal.com, data_extraction_rules.xml excluding sharedpref/database from cloud backup. Ported all 7 iOS models with matching snake_case SerialNames (Food, Kid, Recipe, PlanEntry, GroceryItem, GroceryList, ChatMessage, NutritionInfo) plus enums (FoodCategory, MealSlot, MealResult). Room offline layer mirrors iOS: CachedFood/CachedKid/CachedGroceryItem/PendingMutation entities, CacheDao + PendingMutationDao (observeCount Flow for UI), EatPalDatabase. Services ported: SupabaseClientProvider (Auth/Postgrest/Realtime/Storage/Functions installed, alwaysAutoRefresh + autoLoadFromStorage), AuthService (email/password, Google IDToken equivalent of Apple Sign-In, reset/update/signOut, deleteAccount via functions.invoke('delete-account')), DataService (fetch*/insert*/update*/delete* for foods/kids/recipes/plan_entries/grocery_items + fetchGroceryLists, ensureUserId guardrail + UUID_REGEX validation for plan_entries.kid_id/food_id matching iOS DataServiceError.missingKid/missingFood), OfflineStore (Mutex-gated syncPendingMutations, per-table insert/update replay via kotlinx.serialization, observable pendingMutationCount/isSyncing/lastSyncError StateFlows), RealtimeService (PostgresAction.Insert/Update/Delete flow per channel, fan-out into AppStateStore on* handlers), NetworkMonitor (ConnectivityManager.NetworkCallback + isNetworkError classifier + auto-drain on reconnect). AppStateStore replaces iOS AppState — all 20+ optimistic add/update/delete methods, same rollback-on-failure + enqueue-on-network-error pattern for grocery items. Hilt DataModule, EatPalApplication (Sentry init skipped in debug, NetworkMonitor.start()), MainActivity (@AndroidEntryPoint, single Compose host), RootScreen + RootViewModel (reacts to SessionStatus, kicks loadAllData). README documents stack, build instructions, and the snake_case wire contract as the load-bearing cross-platform guarantee. NOT YET VERIFIED: no `./gradlew assembleDebug` run, no device install. Next-step checklist in follow-up US-201 through US-220.

userStories.description: As an Android user, I want push notifications (meal reminders, family sharing) and local reminders matching iOS behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add firebase-messaging dependency + google-services.json (excluded from VCS; documented in README)

userStories.notes: Android native: SCAFFOLDED 2026-04-22. Local notifications fully wired (previous pass). FCM scaffolding added: firebase-bom 33.7.0 + firebase-messaging-ktx on compile classpath, EatPalMessagingService extends FirebaseMessagingService (@AndroidEntryPoint) — onNewToken routes through PushTokenService, onMessageReceived posts a notification on the channel named in the FCM data payload. PushTokenService (data/remote/PushTokenService.kt) upserts into the shared push_notifications table with the same {user_id, token, platform='android', app_version} shape iOS writes so server senders can target both platforms with one query. Manifest registers the service with the com.google.firebase.MESSAGING_EVENT filter. STILL REQUIRED to activate: (1) add `app/google-services.json` from the Firebase console, (2) add `classpath('com.google.gms:google-services:4.4.2')` to the root build.gradle, (3) apply `id("com.google.gms.google-services")` plugin to `:app`. Without those three steps FirebaseApp never initializes and onNewToken never fires — by design, so the app still builds without a Firebase project configured. Notification-channel-to-UI wiring (per-kid reminder scheduling) is a separate follow-up.

userStories.title: iOS Recipes: live ingredient scaling by serving count in RecipeDetailView
userStories.description: As a parent cooking for a different number of eaters than the recipe was written for, I want to bump servings up/down and see all ingredient quantities scale in real-time so I don't have to do mental math (and so the 'Add ingredients to grocery' action uses the scaled amounts). acceptanceCriteria: Add a Stepper(value: $displayServings, in: 1...20) below the current servings text
Parse `recipe.servings` (currently free-form String like '4' or 'Serves 4-6') with a tolerant regex into `originalServings: Int` — fall back to 1 if unparseable
Compute `scale = displayServings / originalServings`

For each ingredient string in `recipe.additionalIngredients` and any extracted ingredient lines, parse leading quantity (regex on `^(\d+(?:\.\d+)?|\d+/\d+)\s*(\w+)?\s+`) and replace with scaled quantity formatted to fractions where appropriate (½, ¼, ⅓, ⅔, ¾)
Display unscaled original alongside scaled value in muted text when scale ≠ 1.0 ('1 cup → 0.5 cup')
'Add ingredients to grocery' swipe action and context menu in RecipesView.swift:74-100 + 95-115 must respect the scale (add scaled quantities to the grocery item)
Persist last-used scale in @AppStorage keyed by recipe.id so reopening the recipe restores the same scaling
Reset to original servings via long-press on the Stepper or a 'Reset' chip
userStories.notes: iOS: DONE 2026-04-24. Added `Utilities/RecipeScaling.swift` with `parseOriginalServings`, `scaleIngredientsText`, `scaleIngredientLine`, and `formatQuantity`. RecipeDetailView now has a dedicated Servings card (separated from the Meta Info badge row) with a Stepper(1...20), reset button when scaled, and a small '(from <N>)' hint. `additionalIngredients` is rendered scaled when scale ≠ 1.0 with the original shown in tertiary text below. `addIngredientsToGrocery` uses the scale — parsed quantities are scaled via `RecipeScaling.scaleIngredientLine` before being added. Persistence: per-recipe UserDefaults key `recipe.servings.<id>` (0 = use original) restored on onAppear. Quantity parser handles ints, decimals, ASCII fractions (1/2, 3/4), unicode fractions (½¼¾⅓⅔⅛⅜⅝⅞⅕⅖⅗⅘⅙⅚), and mixed forms (1 1/2). Formatter renders back to common unicode fractions when close (±0.03). STILL OPEN: scaling of foodIds-linked ingredients with per-ingredient quantities (requires a recipe_ingredients DB model currently absent), Reset chip separate from the reset button, more tolerant parser on pluralization.

userStories.notes: iOS: DONE 2026-04-24 (AddGroceryItemView only; EditGroceryItemView + VoiceAddGrocerySheet still open). Added `Utilities/GrocerySuggestionEngine.swift` — builds a frequency index from `appState.groceryItems` (primary source of truth; most-recent category/unit/priority per name wins) plus `appState.foods` as a fallback seed when a pantry food has never been added to grocery. Prefix matches outrank substring matches; ties broken by frequency then alphabetically. AddGroceryItemView shows a horizontal chip strip below the name TextField when `suggestions` is non-empty (max 5 chips). Tapping a chip fills name, selects the matching FoodCategory, swaps unit if it's in the whitelisted units list, and sets priority — plus a haptic selection. Suggestions are a computed property recomputed on each body render (acceptable given our data size); the engine itself is O(n) so perf is fine up to thousands of items. STILL OPEN: apply the same engine to EditGroceryItemView (GroceryView.swift:531) and VoiceAddGrocerySheet edit mode, telemetry event (blocked on US-245), debouncing is not needed at current scale but would be if history grows past ~10k.

userStories.description: As an iPhone parent who already tracks my child's growth in the Health app (or via a connected scale / pediatrician portal), I want a one-tap 'Import from Health' button so I'm not retyping numbers I've already logged elsewhere — and so growth-curve data stays in sync. KidProfileEditorView (`ios/EatPal/EatPal/Views/Kids/KidProfileEditorView.swift`) currently requires manual entry; HealthKitService.swift exists but only writes meal-result data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Import from Health' button under the Measurements section (KidProfileEditorView.swift:82)
Extend HealthKitService to request read auth for `dateOfBirthComponents`, `bodyMass`, `height` in `HKObjectType.characteristicType` + quantity types
On tap: show permission sheet if not yet granted, then read latest sample for height/weight and the user's DOB → compute age
Pre-fill `heightCm`, `weightKg`, `age` fields with imported values; show a small Health pill icon next to each field that came from HealthKit
If user edits a Health-imported field, drop the pill (manual override)
Show 'Last synced from Health: <relative date>' caption below the section
Add a settings toggle in HealthSettingsView for 'Auto-refresh from Health on app open'
Graceful no-op if HealthKit not available (iPad without Health, simulator, denied permission) — button hidden with no error

userStories.title: iOS MealPlan: copy a child's plan to another child for a date range
userStories.description: As a parent of multiple kids with overlapping (but not identical) diets, I want to copy Kid A's plan for a date range to Kid B and then tweak the differences, so I'm not rebuilding the whole week. AppState already has `activeKidId` switching but no cross-kid plan duplication action.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Copy to another child' menu item in MealPlanView's existing toolbar Menu (MealPlanView.swift:97)

Sheet: source date range (default = current week), target child picker (excluding active kid), preview of meals to copy (count by slot), allergen-conflict warnings (e.g., 'Sarah is allergic to peanuts; 2 meals will be skipped')
On confirm: create new PlanEntry rows with target kidId, same date+slot+foodId+recipeId, fresh UUIDs, result reset to nil
Skip meals whose food triggers an allergen on the target kid (collect skipped count, show in toast)
Toast: 'Copied 12 of 14 meals to Sarah (2 skipped: peanut allergy)'
Telemetry: `meal_plan_cross_kid_copy` with source_kid, target_kid, date_range_days, copied_count, skipped_count

userStories.title: iOS Pantry: optional expiry date + 'Expiring soon' dashboard card
userStories.description: As a parent fighting food waste, I want to log expiry dates when I add perishables to the pantry, see what's about to expire on the dashboard, and have the AI meal planner prefer expiring ingredients so we use them up. No expiry tracking exists today.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `expiryDate: Date?` to `Food` model (DB migration: `ALTER TABLE foods ADD COLUMN expiry_date DATE`) — generate types via `supabase gen types`
Optional 'Expiry date' DatePicker in AddFoodView and FoodDetailView edit mode (skipped by default; expanded with 'Add expiry')
When ScannedProductView pre-fills from a barcode, default expiry to +7 days (perishable categories) / +30 days (pantry) / nil (other)
Dashboard card 'Expiring this week' shows count of foods with `expiryDate <= now + 7d`; tap → filtered Pantry view
AIMealService.generatePlan accepts an `preferIngredients: [String]` param; populated with expiring foods so the prompt nudges the LLM to plan meals that use them
Pantry row shows red dot + days-until-expiry when ≤ 3 days; struck-through when expired
Push notification (opt-in, daily 9am via NotificationService): 'You have 3 foods expiring in the next 2 days — plan meals?' deep-links to expiring filter

userStories.title: iOS MealPlan: post-meal feedback (1-5 emoji + optional note)
userStories.description: As a parent who logs whether a meal was eaten/tasted/refused, I want a quick optional follow-up — how much did they enjoy it, why did it fail — so the AI planner and 'Most loved' dashboard can learn from real outcomes instead of just binary success/fail.
acceptanceCriteria: After tapping a result button in PlanEntryRow (MealPlanView.swift:429-437) and the existing confirmationDialog choice, show a follow-up sheet (`.sheet(item: ...)` non-blocking) with 1-5 emoji scale + 'Add note' textfield
Skippable — small 'Skip' button + auto-dismiss after 8s if no input
Persist to a new `plan_entry_feedback` table: { id, planEntryId, rating: Int, note: String?, createdAt } with RLS scoped to user_id
Dashboard 'Most loved meals' card sorts by avg rating × frequency, top 5 shown with 1-tap 'Add to plan' action
AIMealService prompt includes top-10 loved + top-5 refused-with-notes so generation reflects real preferences
Progress view adds 'Joy score' chart (avg rating per week, last 8 weeks) alongside variety + outcome charts

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: 'Shopping Mode' one-handed in-store UI
userStories.description: As a parent shopping with one hand on the cart, I want a high-contrast, large-tap-target, dim-but-readable shopping mode that hides checked items so I don't have to scroll past 15 ticked rows to find what I still need. GroceryView is fine for editing but not for in-store use.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle 'Shopping mode' button in GroceryView toolbar (basket-walking icon)
When on: dark forced color scheme override, font scaled 1.4×, list rows ≥ 56pt tall, only unchecked items shown grouped by aisle/category
Single tap anywhere on row toggles checked (not just the small checkbox) with a long satisfying haptic + brief checkmark scale animation
Stays-awake mode: `UIApplication.shared.isIdleTimerDisabled = true` while in shopping mode (auto-clear on exit)
Brightness suggestion banner on first activation: 'Want to lower brightness for low-light aisles?' → one-tap reduces
Live Activity (existing GroceryTripActivityService) shows progress; ending the trip exits shopping mode automatically
Apple Watch mirror: if companion watch app exists (US-237), shopping mode also activates the watch list
Exit button always visible top-right; double-tap any row triggers undo of last check (in case of mis-tap)

userStories.title: iOS AppIntents: expand Siri / Shortcuts coverage beyond meal logging
userStories.description: As a parent multi-tasking in the kitchen or car, I want Siri to do more than just log meal results — adding to grocery, marking foods safe, querying today's plan, and starting AI meal-plan generation should all be voice-driven. Currently only `MealAppIntents.logMealResult` and a Grocery intent are wired.
acceptanceCriteria: New AppIntents in `ios/EatPal/EatPal/Intents/`: `AddGroceryItemIntent` (with @Parameter food name + optional kid), `MarkFoodSafeIntent`, `MarkFoodTryBiteIntent`, `WhatsForDinnerIntent` (returns spoken summary of today's planned meals), `GenerateMealPlanIntent` (kicks off AIMealService for a date)
Each intent provides `IntentDescription`, parameter `@Parameter(title:)` with EntityQuery for food names (so Siri suggests known foods)
App-level `AppShortcutsProvider` registers all intents with natural-language phrases ('Add milk to my EatPal grocery list', 'What's for dinner', 'Mark broccoli as a try-bite for Leo')
Each intent returns `IntentResult & ProvidesDialog` with spoken confirmation and (where applicable) `OpensIntent` for follow-up tap
Intents donate to system on first successful use so they appear in the lock-screen Smart Stack and Spotlight
All intents respect signed-in / kid-selected state — fail with conversational error ('First sign in to EatPal') rather than crashing
Add a Settings → Voice & Shortcuts section listing every intent with example phrases + 'Add to Siri' inline button

userStories.title: iOS Sync: pending-mutation indicator + manual 'Sync now' UX
userStories.description: As an offline-first user, I want a visible indicator when changes are queued locally and a manual 'Sync now' button so I'm not guessing whether my last 5 edits made it to the cloud. OfflineStore already queues PendingMutation rows but the UX is invisible.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab-bar overlay badge on the active tab when `OfflineStore.pendingMutationCount > 0` (subtle dot + count)
Tap badge → 'Pending changes' sheet listing each queued mutation with type, target name, queued-at relative time, and last-attempt error (if any)
'Sync now' button in that sheet retries the queue immediately (currently runs on app foreground only)
Settings → Sync section: last successful sync timestamp, queue depth, button to clear failed mutations after manual review
Toast 'Synced 5 changes' when queue drains naturally; persistent banner at top of screen if a mutation fails 3+ times ('1 grocery edit couldn't sync — tap to review')
App-launch hook: if pending mutations exist on cold start, attempt drain in background before allowing edits (or allow edits but warn)
Test: airplane mode → make 5 edits → toggle wifi → badge clears, toast appears

userStories.title: iOS MealPlan: pre-built starter template library
userStories.description: As a brand-new user staring at an empty meal plan, I want to pick from a small library of curated weekly templates ('Balanced Toddler Week', 'Texture Variety Week', 'Nut-Free Family Week', 'Budget-Friendly') and apply one as a starting point I then tweak. MealPlanTemplateService already supports save/load of user templates but ships with none.
acceptanceCriteria: Bundle 5-8 hand-curated templates as JSON in `Resources/StarterMealPlans.json` (plan name + 7 days × 4 slots × food names + descriptive blurb + appropriate-age-range)
Empty-state of MealPlanView shows 'Start from a template' button alongside 'Add manually' and the existing AI generator
Template browser sheet: cards with name, blurb, week preview, 'Use this' button
On apply: foods that match by name are linked; foods that don't exist in user's pantry are auto-created (with `addedVia: 'starter_template'`) so the plan references real Food rows
Allergen check before applying — warn if active kid is allergic to ≥1 ingredient ('This template uses peanut butter. Continue, swap, or cancel?')
Templates show in same browser as user-saved templates with a 'Curated' chip distinguishing them
Telemetry: `starter_template_applied` with template_name + foods_created_count

userStories.title: iOS AICoach: empty / loading / error state coverage
userStories.description: As a user opening AI Coach when network is slow or down, I want to see a clear loading skeleton, an actionable error if the request fails, and a friendly empty state on first use — instead of an indefinitely blank chat view that looks broken.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty-state on first conversation: friendly hero card with 3-5 example prompt chips ('Why won't my kid eat broccoli?', 'Plan a nut-free dinner', 'Ideas for picky eaters') — tap inserts the prompt
While AI is responding: typing-indicator dots + 'EatPal is thinking…' (already partially exists — verify; if missing, add)
On AICoachService failure: inline error message in the conversation thread (not a toast) with 'Retry' button on the failed user message
Network-down state: top banner 'Offline — AI Coach needs internet' with a queued-question hint
Long-pending requests (>15s): cancel button appears in the typing indicator
VoiceOver: typing indicator announces 'EatPal is generating a response'; failed message is labeled 'Failed — double-tap to retry'

userStories.title: iOS watchOS companion app: today's plan + grocery checklist
userStories.description: As an Apple Watch wearer, I want a tiny companion app that shows today's planned meals and lets me check off grocery items from my wrist while shopping or cooking — without pulling out my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: New `ios/EatPal/EatPalWatch/` watchOS target sharing the existing `ios/EatPal/Shared/` models
Two screens: Today (list of today's PlanEntry rows grouped by slot, tap → spoken meal name + log-result complication), Grocery (unchecked items, tap to check, swipe to delete)
WatchConnectivity: phone pushes a snapshot of today's plan + grocery on each AppState change; watch can push grocery-checked deltas back
Two complications: small 'Tonight: <dish>' (Modular Small / Corner), large 'Grocery: X / Y items' (Modular Large)
Watch app falls back to a 'Open EatPal on iPhone' state if no snapshot has been received yet
Activate Live Activity from the watch (start grocery trip from wrist)
Telemetry: watch_action_count by surface (today vs grocery vs complication)

userStories.title: iOS AI: 'Plan from photo of fridge' meal generator path
userStories.description: As a parent staring into a fridge wondering what to cook, I want to snap a photo of my fridge contents and have the AI generate a meal plan that uses what I have on hand (and tell me what's missing). ImageTextRecognizer already exists — extend it for fridge contents recognition or pipe the photo to the existing vision-capable AIMealService.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Plan from fridge' button in AIMealPlanView next to the existing prompt entry

Camera or photo-library picker → photo passed to a new `FridgeRecognitionService` that calls a new edge function `recognize-fridge-contents` (multimodal LLM) returning a list of recognized items + confidence
Confirmation sheet: editable list of detected items (user can tag missing items, remove false positives) before generation
AIMealService.generatePlan called with `availableIngredients: [String]` so the prompt biases toward using on-hand items
After generation: render plan + a 'You'll also need' grocery list of items the plan needs but the fridge photo didn't include
One-tap 'Add missing to grocery list' button
Telemetry: `ai_plan_from_fridge` with detected_count, used_in_plan_count, missing_count

userStories.title: iOS Settings: notification preferences UI
userStories.description: As a user who either gets too many or too few push notifications, I want a settings panel where I can toggle per-topic and pick a preferred time-of-day for each. NotificationService.swift exists but there's no visible preferences UI today.
acceptanceCriteria: New SettingsView section 'Notifications' with topic toggles: Meal-plan reminders, Grocery list ready, Try-bite achieved, Expiring food (US-230), AI weekly summary, Streak milestones (US-241 if shipped)
Per-topic optional time picker (default: respect quiet hours)
'Test notification' button per topic that fires a sample immediately so user can preview content
Settings persisted to UserDefaults + mirrored to Supabase `notification_preferences` table so cross-device install respects them
Single 'Mute all notifications for 24h' chip at top of section
On first launch (post-onboarding) prompt for permission contextually, not at app open ('Want a reminder when your grocery list is ready?')
Each scheduled notification's payload includes a `topic` field so analytics can attribute opens

userStories.title: iOS Quiz: surface PickyEaterQuizView from Kids/Settings/Dashboard
userStories.description: PickyEaterQuizView exists in the codebase but has no entry point in the production UI. Parents complete the quiz on the web but iOS users never see it. Surface it so quiz-driven personalization (pickinessLevel + texture/flavor preferences) is reachable from the device.
acceptanceCriteria: Entry point 1: KidProfileEditorView footer card 'Take the picky-eater quiz' → opens the quiz with active kid context
Entry point 2: Dashboard onboarding nudge if kid has `pickinessLevel == nil` ('Personalize meal suggestions in 2 minutes')
Entry point 3: Settings → Children → per-kid 'Retake quiz' button
Quiz writes results back via KidUpdate (pickinessLevel, texturePreferences, flavorPreferences, behavioralNotes) — uses the same KidUpdate path as KidProfileEditorView
Result screen shows a 'Profile insights' summary with 3-4 plain-language sentences ('Sarah tends to refuse mixed textures — try serving components separately')
Telemetry: quiz_started, quiz_completed (with pickinessLevel result), quiz_abandoned_at_question_n
Cross-platform parity: quiz_completed event keyed the same on web/iOS/Android so funnel works across surfaces

userStories.title: iOS Progress: try-bite streaks + milestone badges
userStories.description: As a parent (and as a kid I show the screen to), I want streaks and unlockable badges to celebrate progress — first try-bite, 5-bite streak, tried 5 colors, mastered a category — to keep motivation high. Progress view shows raw stats but no celebration.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a `Badge` enum + JSON catalog (10-15 badges at launch) with criteria functions — e.g., `firstTryBite`, `fiveDayStreak`, `triedFiveColors`, `vegetableExplorer` (10 distinct veg)
On every PlanEntry result update, evaluate criteria and persist newly-earned badges to a new `kid_badges` table { kidId, badgeId, earnedAt }
ProgressDashboardView: 'Streak' card showing current streak + best-ever streak; 'Badges' grid showing earned vs locked
Earning a badge triggers a celebratory sheet (Lottie or simple confetti) with optional Share button (image-only export, no PII)
Streaks reset on a recorded `refused` result (not on absence of data — missed days don't break streak)
Per-kid streaks (so multi-kid households don't accidentally share)
Toast feedback: 'New badge: Vegetable Explorer 🥦' deep-links to badge detail

userStories.title: iOS Household: family-share via iCloud Family or invite code
userStories.description: As a household with two parents (and grandparents, nannies, ex-partners), I want multiple adults to view + edit the same meal plan, pantry, and grocery list without sharing one login. No multi-adult support exists today.
acceptanceCriteria: DB: introduce `household_id` on user_subscriptions, plan_entries, foods, recipes, grocery_items, kids; backfill default per existing user (each user is a household of 1)
RLS: SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies use `household_id IN (SELECT household_id FROM household_members WHERE user_id = auth.uid())` instead of `user_id = auth.uid()`
Settings → Household: 'Invite family member' generates a 6-char code valid 24h; 'Join household' field on opposite end
Members list with role (Owner / Editor / Viewer) and revoke
Realtime: household-scoped channels so all members see live updates
Migration UX: existing solo users see no change unless they invite someone
Conflict UX: if Editor A and Editor B both edit the same grocery item within 5s, last-write-wins with a one-time toast 'Sarah also edited this — kept your version'
iCloud Family integration optional — invite-code path is the source of truth so non-Apple-Family households still work

userStories.title: iOS Budget: meal-cost calculator + weekly spend forecast
userStories.description: As a budget-conscious parent, I want EatPal to estimate the cost of my meal plan + grocery list for the week so I can decide whether to swap to cheaper meals before shopping. Web has BudgetCalculator + BudgetAnalyticsDashboard; iOS has neither.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional `pricePerUnit: Double?` and `currency: String?` to Food + GroceryItem models (DB migration + types regen)
When adding to grocery, optional 'Price' field; when scanning a barcode, fetch from OpenFoodFacts price field if present
New Budget tab (or Settings entry) with: weekly forecast (sum of priced grocery items + plan-derived ingredients), price-per-meal breakdown, weekly target setting
AIMealService accepts optional `weeklyBudget: Double?` and biases toward cheaper meals when set
Pantry view shows total inventory value (sum of priced foods) when prices known
Currency follows device locale; no manual currency conversion (keep raw values per item, display in Locale.current.currency.identifier)
Soft-fail when prices unknown ('Add prices to 12 items to see weekly forecast' empty state with shortcut to bulk-price flow)

userStories.title: iOS Pantry: bulk-add via paste / multi-line text input
userStories.description: As a parent migrating from another app or importing a shopping list someone texted me, I want to paste a list of foods ('milk, dairy\nbroccoli, vegetable\nchicken breast, protein') and have them all created in one shot — instead of opening AddFoodView 20 times.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Bulk add' menu option in Pantry toolbar (next to existing barcode + add)

Sheet with a multi-line TextEditor and a parser that supports 3 formats: comma-per-line ('name, category'), one-name-per-line (defaults category to 'other'), and ingredient-list with quantities ('2 cups milk', '1 lb chicken')
Live preview of parsed rows with editable category dropdown per row before commit
'Auto-detect category' uses a small lookup table (keyword → category) so 'milk' → dairy, 'chicken' → protein, etc.
Commit creates Food rows in a single batched OfflineStore.enqueue call so offline still works
Toast: 'Added 24 foods' with undo button (tap → bulk-delete the just-created IDs)
Reuse the same parser as AICoach 'extract foods from this' helper so behavior is consistent

userStories.title: iOS Analytics: event-coverage gap fill
userStories.description: Sentry breadcrumbs are wired but feature-usage events are spotty — we can't see which entry surfaces drive food creation, recipe imports, AI plan generation, or grocery completion. Fill the gaps so PM can see funnels.
acceptanceCriteria: Standardize event names in a new `Services/Analytics.swift` enum (e.g., `.foodAdded(via:)`, `.recipeImported(source:)`, `.mealResultLogged(result:)`, `.aiPlanGenerated(promptType:)`, `.groceryItemChecked(method:)`)
Fire events at every CRUD boundary in AppState (1 line per method)
Screen-view events on `.task {}` of every tab + each major sheet
Standard properties on every event: kid_id (hashed), is_offline, app_version, locale
PII never sent — event payloads run through a small redactor that strips names + emails before send
Sentry dashboards (or PostHog if added) split by event for the top 10 events; doc the dashboard URLs in docs/analytics.md

userStories.title: iOS Accessibility: VoiceOver + dynamic-type audit pass
userStories.description: As a user relying on VoiceOver or large text sizes, I want every interactive element labeled and every layout to reflow gracefully when dynamic-type is at AX5. Current coverage is partial — a structured audit + fix pass is needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit checklist: every Button/Image-as-button has `.accessibilityLabel`; every interactive icon has a hint; complex composite cards use `.accessibilityElement(children: .combine)` with a single descriptive label
Test all main views at Dynamic Type sizes XS, M, XL, AX1, AX5 — capture screenshots, file follow-up tickets for any clipped/overlapping content
VoiceOver test of the 5 critical flows: add food, add grocery, plan a meal, log a meal result, scan a barcode — each flow completable end-to-end without sight
Reduce-motion respected on all SwiftUI animations (gate `.spring()` etc. behind `if !UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled`)
Color contrast check: AppTheme tokens audited against WCAG AA 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text
VoiceOver smoke test included in CI snapshot suite (where feasible)
ACCESSIBILITY.md doc capturing the audit, fixes, and known-good baseline

userStories.title: iOS UX consistency: navigation pattern + error toast standardization
userStories.description: Audit and lock in the iOS navigation pattern (sheet vs push vs fullScreenCover) so users develop a stable mental model, and replace generic 'Error' toasts with actionable, contextual messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Document the rule: half-sheets for quick add/edit, full-screen-cover for camera/scanner only, NavigationStack push for hierarchical content (recipe detail, kid profile)
Sweep `.sheet` and `.fullScreenCover` usages against the rule; fix outliers — captured as a small PR per Views/ subfolder
Define `AppError` enum in `Utilities/AppError.swift` with cases (network, validation(field:), offline, server(code:), unknown) + `userMessage` and `recoverySuggestion`
Wrap DataService throws in AppError so toasts show actionable messages: 'No internet — your change is saved and will sync when online' instead of 'Error'
ToastManager.shared.error gains an optional action/handler so toasts can offer 'Retry' for transient errors
Offline mutations never show error toasts (queued silently — see US-234 sync indicator instead)
Add Storybook-style preview file `Utilities/Previews/ErrorStates.swift` showing every AppError in light + dark + reduce-motion variants for designer review
